profile.name: Siyuan
profile.bio:
Hi! My name is Siyuan.
I love open landscapes, cycling with the wind, adventures, and unexpected discoveries ✨

quote.side:
“One’s life has value so long as one attributes value to the life of others, by means of love, friendship, indignation and compassion.”
— Simone de Beauvoir

about.longBio: In my spare time, I love tidying up my room, oil painting, and cooking. On weekends, you’ll usually find me going for walks, cycling to catch the breeze, or working up a good sweat playing badminton. I enjoy humor and am always drawn to fun, witty, and creative ideas.
about.photoAlt: Portrait of Siyuan Cai in a garden walkway

lifeMapping.ambientLine1: You can't connect the dots looking forward；
lifeMapping.ambientLine2: you can only connect them looking backward.
lifeMapping.heading: Dots.

dotsPhilosophy.title: On connecting the dots
paragraphs: I am a huge believer in Steve Jobs’ philosophy of {{connectingDots}}.

So I want to introduce myself starting from the dots I’ve encountered along the way—through memorable experiences, moments of excitement, and sparks of curiosity that helped me rediscover who I am. I’m also genuinely curious about what shape those dots might form when I look back one day—whether they become a line, a network, or perhaps a river that branches out and eventually converges again.

This idea of connecting the dots has also changed the way I see things. I’ve come to believe that most of the dots in life don’t come with an instruction manual. They simply exist to be believed in, to be felt, to be lived through. Even interests or impulses that don’t seem useful at the time may eventually find their place in the future. I’m learning to meet each dot with a more open perspective, to look at every experience on more equal footing, and to see the more constructive side of what has happened, while trying to understand how these experiences might be connected.
connectingDotsHoverSegments: Many experiences seem scattered and useless in the moment, but only when you look back, do you realize they were connected all along, pointing toward the same outcome.

workBlocks.heading: AI News Platform — Content Operations (AKA AI Content Discovery)

bodyParagraphs.text: Participated in building an AI news aggregation platform, responsible for content system development and overall content quality control.

Defined editorial positioning and iterated content formats (text news, audio-visual media, social media); established evaluation criteria and content selection frameworks; tracked and curated high-quality global AI information sources to build a stable intelligence acquisition network.
Participated in operations of an AI-powered read-later product, responsible for product cold start execution and copywriting.

Acquired initial seed users, collected and structured user feedback, facilitated requirement organization and product iteration, and established a closed-loop user communication and feedback mechanism; also responsible for brand content production and product messaging support.

exp-02.title: ModelBest & Tsinghua NLP Lab — AI Technical Content Operations & Open-Source Community Operations

titleSegments.text: — AI Technical Content Operations & Open-Source Community Operations

workBlocks.heading: Official WeChat Account Lead, Tsinghua NLP Laboratory

bodyParagraphs.text: Large-Model Technology Communication: Edited and disseminated academic AI content; participated in releases of key projects including ChatDev and the MiniCPM series.
Visual Design:
Designed UI for the laboratory recruitment webpage
; built visual identity for the official account platform from scratch (0→1).

workBlocks.heading: OpenBMB Matrix Media Operations & Open-Source Community Operations

bodyParagraphs.text: Social Media Communication: Daily operation, publishing, and engagement management across Chinese platforms (Xiaohongshu, Jike, Weibo, Bilibili) and
X
(Twitter).
Campaign Support: Coordinated social media communication rhythms for major events such as technical releases and
large-model public courses
Technical Community Management: Maintained OpenBMB open-source communities across WeChat, Discord, and other platforms.
Open-Source Award Applications: Led submissions for recognitions including “
2024 Open Source Community of the Year
” and “QbitAI AI 100 Products”.
Assisted Prof. Tan Wenyong in compiling and editing
Urban Morphological Elements
; contributed to the writing and illustrations of Chapter 2 and supported proofreading for the entire volume.
An Empirical Study on Measurement Methods of Road Network Morphology in Mountainous Residential Areas: A Case Study of the Old Urban District of Chongqing
— Nominated for Outstanding Graduation Thesis.
Strategies for Building Resilience of 15-Minute Community Life Circles from the Perspective of Pandemic
, published in
International Journal of Environmental Impacts
Optimization Strategies for Community Public Service Facilities in the Post-Pandemic Era: A Case Study of Zhongxinwan Community, Shijingpo, Chongqing
, included in the proceedings of the
Environmental Behavior Society
